seoTitle: Burrup vs Burswood — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Burrup and Burswood. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Burrup and Burswood.
heading: Burrup vs Burswood — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Burrup, Western Australia and Burswood, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Burrup has a lower overall climate risk score (20/100) compared to Burswood (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Burrup or Burswood safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Burrup has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Burswood scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Burrup scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Burswood scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Burrup or Burswood?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Burrup has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
